beats.title: Orden's Plea
beats.boundaryReason: The opening dinner scene at Trok's shack establishes the missing-daughter hook and ends with the plan to seek Ryu at breakfast.
dateEvidence: The transcript opens in the evening in Hamri.
The episode note places Orden's request on 1747-10-23.

beats.title: Breakfast at Ryu's
beats.boundaryReason: The next morning the scene shifts to Ryu's tower, where the group recruits him and meets Nerissa.
dateEvidence: The transcript explicitly says 'the next morning'.
The scene is framed as breakfast at Ryu's tower.

beats.title: Rumors at the Leviathan
beats.boundaryReason: The party moves into town, questions Abelard at the Leviathan, and confirms the upriver lead before setting out.
dateEvidence: The tavern visit continues the same morning as breakfast.
The episode note places the Abelard rumor-gathering on 1747-10-24.

beats.title: Up the Mirmir
beats.boundaryReason: The party leaves Hamri, tracks Hiyasa upriver, makes camp, and discovers her abandoned campsite on the following day.
dateEvidence: The party leaves town after the morning tavern stop.
The transcript includes an overnight camp and a resumed search the next day.

beats.title: The Stone Monument
beats.boundaryReason: The search culminates in finding the buried monument of Yerkir-khor, reading its inscriptions, and descending into the complex.
dateEvidence: The episode note places arrival at the tomb on 1747-10-26.
The scene is framed as reaching the structure after the upriver pursuit.

beats.title: Crystal Halls
beats.boundaryReason: Inside the monument, the party navigates the glittering chambers, traps, and statues before the first major fight fully begins.
dateEvidence: This scene immediately follows the descent into the tomb.
No rest or day transition occurs between the monument entry and interior exploration.

beats.title: Gargoyle Ambush
beats.boundaryReason: The chamber encounter resolves into a contained combat with gargoyles and a snake-headed statue, ending when the enemies are broken and examined.
dateEvidence: The transcript shifts from trap-solving to initiative and repeated attack turns.
The fight concludes before the party resumes searching deeper into the tomb.

beats.title: Deeper into the Tomb
beats.boundaryReason: After the first fight, the party heals, loots, bypasses more traps, and follows muddy footprints toward the inner sanctum.
dateEvidence: This section is continuous tomb exploration after the gargoyle fight.
The muddy tracks and trap navigation lead directly toward the final chamber.

beats.title: The Guardian's Terms
beats.boundaryReason: The party reaches the inner tomb, finds Hiyasa trapped in stone, and unsuccessfully tries to satisfy the guardian's demand for tribute.
dateEvidence: The transcript reveals Hiyasa in the final chamber before hostilities begin.
Negotiation and tribute discussion precede the combat turns.

beats.title: Guardian Battle Begins
beats.boundaryReason: The guardian rejects the party's offerings and the first phase of the final battle establishes the enemy's stone-and-earth powers.
dateEvidence: The guardian repeats its tribute demand and combat starts immediately after.
This section contains the opening rounds before the battlefield shifts again.

beats.title: Cracking the Colossus
beats.boundaryReason: The middle phase of the fight focuses on sustained damage, psychic and thunder effects, and the party's attempt to wear the guardian down.
dateEvidence: This section is continuous combat with no scene break.
The mud transformation has not yet locked the room into the final crisis.

beats.title: Mud and Rescue
beats.boundaryReason: The guardian turns the floor to mud, nearly entombs Trok, and is finally destroyed when Ryu turns the battle.
dateEvidence: The transcript explicitly marks the floor turning to mud.
This phase ends only once the guardian is defeated and the immediate danger passes.

beats.title: Return with the Spoils
beats.boundaryReason: The final aftermath covers questioning Hiyasa, looting the tomb, and a later return to Hamri where the treasure and scrolls are divided.
dateEvidence: The tomb aftermath begins immediately after the guardian's defeat.
The transcript later jumps ahead to the inn a few days later, matching the return on 1747-10-28 in the episode note.
